sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available in sources

special_mechanics.name: Sword Combat
special_mechanics.description: Player obtains sword in level 1. Combat maneuvers include: advance, back off, slash, parry, and combined parry-then-slash attack. Enemy swordsmen have health indicators similar to protagonist.

special_mechanics.name: Magic Mirror
special_mechanics.description: Unique trap in level 4 that forces protagonist to jump through. Doppelgänger emerges and drains protagonist's health to one.

special_mechanics.name: Fall Damage
special_mechanics.description: Falling from two or more floors of height causes loss of one health indicator.

special_mechanics.name: Instant Death Traps
special_mechanics.description: Spike traps, deep pits (three or more stories deep), and guillotines result in instant death.

special_mechanics.name: Gate Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Gates can be raised for short periods by standing on activation trigger. Player must pass through while open.

special_mechanics.name: Doppelgänger Merge
special_mechanics.description: In level 12, protagonist battles shadow doppelgänger. They share health pool - any damage hurts both. Must merge to proceed.

special_mechanics.name: Potions
special_mechanics.description: Red potions restore one health indicator. Blue potions are poisonous and take one health indicator as damage. Large red potions increase maximum health by one. Large green potions grant temporary hover ability.

tier_notes: Tier 2 data: SNES version features 10 additional levels beyond original 12 (total 22 levels), 120-minute time limit vs 60 minutes in original. No specific numerical physics parameters documented in available sources. Game designed by Jordan Mechner using rotoscoping technique from brother's acrobatic reference footage.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Prince of Persia SNES article)
wikipedia - Prince of Persia (1989 video game)
